claim: UNICEF reports global under-five mortality fell from 93 to 37 per 1000 live births between 1990 and 2022, but the poorest-richest mortality ratio exceeds 2:1 in over 30 LMICs and sub-national inequality widened in many countries.

source.title: Levels and Trends in Child Mortality 2024

method: Reviewed UNICEF child mortality report, inequality analysis, and wealth-disaggregated data.
limitations: Sub-national mortality estimates are modeled and have wide confidence intervals for small districts.
Wealth-disaggregated data depends on DHS and MICS survey frequency.

claim: A 2020 Lancet Global Health study found that sub-national inequality in child mortality widened in over 40 percent of LMICs between 2000 and 2015, even as national averages declined, with the poorest districts showing the slowest improvement.

source.title: Subnational inequalities in under-five mortality in low- and middle-income countries

method: Reviewed sub-national inequality analysis, trend methodology, and wealth-disaggregated findings.
limitations: Inequality measurement depends on survey frequency and district sample sizes.
Study period ends in 2015 and may not reflect recent trends.
